title: Enforce transport-safe call shapes before execution
goal: Enforce transport-safe boundaries on generic synchronous terminal and fleet execution before process start, route shell-composed and known indirect work to durable tasks, keep generic synchronous execution under server-owned time and output limits, route predictably large reads through typed or split-read surfaces, and prove the behavior through regression, CI and live deployment evidence without claiming that timeout parameters caused the ChatGPT UI hang.

acceptance.assertion: Task metadata preserves live-register event 278 and candidate candidate-0379aae87f6f4730975e37f7e6554c88; the event remains observation evidence and does not itself authorize execution or merge.
grabowski_terminal_run, grabowski_fleet_run and the generic Fleet CLI evaluate the same deterministic call-shape gate before any local or SSH process starts.

Generic synchronous execution no longer accepts caller-selected timeout or output-limit parameters. The runtime owns a 30-second timeout and 64-KiB output ceiling and preserves timeout termination as a process-safety mechanism.

Shell composition and known indirect or detaching launchers are rejected with process_started=false, bounded reason codes and required_route=durable_task. The success contract advertises typed_read_or_split_read for predictably large reads while the executor itself enforces and may truncate at 64 KiB rather than claiming advance knowledge of output size.
Typed internal probes and persistent task/job executors retain their own bounded contracts and are not accidentally forced through the generic synchronous ceiling.

Existing mutation transport ambiguity semantics remain fail-closed: an interrupted response cannot authorize an unchanged mutation retry, and target-state readback remains required before another mutation attempt.

Tests cover direct success, direct and embedded shells, known wrapper and detaching launchers, Fleet CLI parity, denial before _run, fixed server-owned limits, plain shell and wrapper names used only as data, unchanged tool names and stale-client schema rejection before process execution.
Focused tests, the complete test and contract suite, required GitHub CI and a head- and diff-bound critical self-review pass on the unchanged published PR head.

The merge head is deployed and live probes prove allowed bounded Terminal and Fleet calls plus fail-closed shell and indirect-launcher denials before process start, with the new server-owned contract returned on successful calls.

Documentation and receipts state that the gate mitigates avoidable long synchronous transport exposure but does not establish the root cause of the ChatGPT application hang or guarantee that all future platform hangs are impossible.

validation.full_local_validation: 1324 tests passed; policy, generated context, publication profiles, runtime lock, deploy tooling and secret scan passed

deployment.integrity: all deployment identity and provenance flags valid

live_smoke.terminal_direct: passed with server_owned_limits=true, timeout_seconds=30 and max_output_bytes=65536
live_smoke.terminal_shell: denied before process start with shell_composition_requires_durable_task
live_smoke.terminal_indirect: denied before process start with indirect_execution_requires_durable_task
live_smoke.fleet_direct: passed with the same server-owned synchronous contract
live_smoke.fleet_shell: denied before host execution with shell_composition_requires_durable_task
live_smoke.connector_snapshot: the current chat still advertises the old optional fields; calls using only the retained required fields reach the new runtime, while a fresh connector snapshot is required to display the new schema

metadata.verification_note: PR #170 merged, exact merge head deployed, runtime identity and provenance valid, direct and denied call shapes exercised live, and the task wording corrected to distinguish runtime output bounding from preflight split-read routing.
does_not_establish: semantic behavior of every arbitrary direct executable
fresh connector schema in the current chat
